title: Silky Turkish Chestnut Iskender
description: A nutty, silky, light Turkish dish featuring roasted chestnuts, double-rise bread, and a hint of spices. Serve warm, garnished with fresh parsley for a delicious breakfast or brunch.

ingredients.name: Chestnuts
ingredients.amount: 1 cup (120g) peeled and chopped fresh or frozen chestnuts

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chopped chestnuts and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until tender and lightly browned.
Season with salt, black pepper, and white pepper, to taste.
Toast the double-rise bread slices until lightly browned and crispy.
To assemble, place a toasted bread slice on a serving plate, followed by a spoonful of the roasted chestnut mixture.
Repeat with the remaining bread slices and chestnut mixture, finishing with a bread slice on top.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm, directly from the skillet.
For a crisper top, place the assembled iskender under the broiler for 1-2 minutes, until lightly browned and golden.
